channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Started buying USO calls in January when it became obvious we were gonna do something stupid with Iran. — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Started buying USO calls in January when it became obvious we were gonna do something stupid with Iran.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-08 20:52 UTC

OP

Started buying USO calls in January when it became obvious we were gonna do something stupid with Iran. Lost like $2k when the Feb calls expired but now I’m beginning to feel like I won the lotto. Could end up being a life-changing amount of money. Current plan is to hold either until end of this week or next Wednesday. After that I’m considering waiting until the oil markets stabilize and then buying a large amount of SCO.

Thank you retard Monke Drumpf

Early replies (first 3)

61949476
>>61949287
finally..one of us made it

61949578
>>61949287
Nice pamp brother, we're all gonna make it.

61949593
28k is life changing money to you? What part of the third world do you hail from?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61949603
>>61949593
Fuck off. Most folks are just getting by. $28k can help someone substantially.

Congrats anon, I've been playing with USO as well this week but I got in later.

61949608
>>61949593
Do the math on what the calls will be worth if WTI hits ~$150-$200 over the next two weeks.

61949624
>>61949603
This is the first time that obsessively following world events is actually paying off for me. I am so tempted to just move whatever I walk away with into SCO once things stabilize, but surely it can’t be that easy

61949654
>>61949287
Trump announces bad news on Friday night so the market won't be rattled, and he announces good news on Sunday before the market opens. He wants oil prices down and he knew what the Iran response would be to the attack before he did it. You might get caught with your pants down tomorrow.

61949690
>>61949654
Possible, but I am willing to be that the oil crisis doesn’t get resolved this week. Much more likely that it deepens.

61949694
>>61949690
*willing to bet

61949717
>>61949690
The US blowing up Iran's oil supply won't help prices come down. Since it's just for their own domestic use, that cuts off their exports since they now need to rely on that source.

61949732
>>61949287
i did the same but with UCO beacuse its atually tracks oil more accurately and i lost because trump kept delayng the attacks like a bitch

61949739
>>61949654
>he announces good news on Sunday before the market opens
there are no good news

61952864
>>61949287
OP, newfag here, how do I open an account for it?

item.title: I put roughly $400k on USO puts at strike price $90 expiring march 27th. — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I put roughly $400k on USO puts at strike price $90 expiring march 27th.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-08 20:44 UTC

OP

I put roughly $400k on USO puts at strike price $90 expiring march 27th.

I did xanax and blacked out and placed the trades and fell asleep. it's my entire account and my net liquidity is like $13k or something.

so hopefully oil go down a lil bit or else im gonna have a heart attack on monday. i havent eaten and I still have no appetite. i cant even post pictures of the positions (im already down a bit on them)

I wish I was kidding, I cant feel my face and I start screaming every now and then. I already had the police called on me because the neighbors got worried and I explained it to the cops and they just went quiet and wished me luck.

I tried calling the exchange but there was nothing they could do.

Early replies (first 3)

61950275
Are you retarded? Rhetorical question. Sell them at open and take the idiot tax

61950614
>I explained it to the cops and they just went quiet and wished me luck

Cops don’t know what options are.

61950653
>>61950166
This is a larp until you post postions

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950671
its gongo up

61950686
>>61950166
I have USO $150 puts expiring 12/18. But I only put $1700 lol. Starting to think it's a bad idea, oil fields are on fire so oil might pop off on Monday. Might be a good time to buy puts on Monday though, can't imagine any news being worst than oil fields on fire in Iran

Also wtf is up with the captcha. They are hard

61952836
wow i cant breathe

61952840
For once in my stupid fucking life I did not sell too early

item.title: ZOOM OUT — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ZOOM OUT

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-08 20:54 UTC

OP

ZOOM OUT

61952869
saar

61952871
>>61952865
So buy SOL?

item.title: There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-08 20:45 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61952823
>>61952797
is this another one of those tricks where India becomes Southern Asian becomes Eastern Europe?

61952844
>>61952797
Ok, but why are both decreasing?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: It is blatantly obvious that the US will lose this war — 98 replies — 82.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
It is blatantly obvious that the US will lose this war

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-03-08 19:43 UTC

OP

And that's a nothingburger

Early replies (first 3)

61942258
????? It means we bomb the shit out of them wdym

61942268
>>61942181
I hate being American over the last decade so much, all we do is lose and embarrass ourselves... how the fuck can we spend so much money and effort and lose a war with a country that has no resources? Meanwhile China will do nothing and win again and I will gave to endure all of 4chan laughing at us... I want to support China but I can't into Chinese women. Europe needs to become a superpower and dethrone China and America so I can cheer them on without reservations.

61942402
>>61942268
>how the fuck can we spend so much money and effort and lose a war with a country that has no resources?
Bombing a mountain just creates a field

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61948084
>>61946430
>so you're astroturfing the most retarded blackpill-takes i've ever seen for free?
I am white but I am a poorfag loser it's true. Not trying to astroturf anything, that's genuinely how I feel about it.

61948208
>>61943069
there are american ones too but their job isnt to shill, its to create conflict between us poors so that we dont eat the rich. all the tranny posting is by them.

61948661
>>61942181
Around 2011 Stefan Molyneux, libertarians and the tea party were saying the US Government was in $14 trillion of debt and there is no way the forever wars can continue. Look at us now.

https://odysee.com/@freedomain:b/another-imperial-quagmire-dr-ivan-eland:c

61948695
>>61943665
What an inane schizo rant. There is not a single indication for this to be true.

61948804
>>61942181
I voted for this

61948817
>>61943056
Germany declared war on the USA and not the other way around

61948937
>>61943665
>theories jewish psychologists invented, and retarded pseuds peddle to try and poison the well of Hitler-sentiment to keep the goys from waking up.
JlDF trannies are like broken records i swear, did they just replace you freaks with AI or something? hard to tell.

>>61948817
on paper they did, because the USA baited Japan, their ally, into attacking them, by blockading their cargo ships so they couldn't source crude oil. I could personally declare war against the USA right now from new zealand, but its as meaningless as when HitIer did.

>>61948661
alot of geopolitical and finance bros outside the normie sphere thought the same thing, but we were all blinded by one problem, every single US president and all the top officials were all controlled ped0s and puppets for globohomo. and on top of that, they were all retarded too.

But nobody realised HOW retarded and corrupt, because a relatively normal man with a good head for old-school economics, stepped in and simply copied what has worked in the past for the USA, and in his personal life. Its not a scenario that anyone could have predicted.

Trump is potentially solving the national debt by taxing the rest of the planet to do it. It'll probably work too, for awhile, until the rest of us go broke because of it. The central banking carteI designed this system to fail, not work for everyone.

61949034
used to browse 4chan all the time. old /pol/ etc yada who cares. fuck me this place looks dated. even if 99% of people here were working for some government agency or another - it's still just a rss feed for twitter which stopped mattering 8 years ago. who the fuck even bothers shilling on 4chan in 2028-2?

61949083
>>61943623
You are the midwit that believed your history teacher when he divided the class between non blond and blued eyed people vs everyone else and told that Hitler would kill everyone on the non blond/blue eyed side

61949561
>>61942633
Trump is a lot smarter than people give him credit for, and that is bad thing..
Don't get me wrong, i don't think there is any plan, most of it just seems to be throwing spaghetti at the wall and see what sticks.
..and "what sticks" - a desired outcome, seems to be either money (for him), elite loyalty, and/or creating prestige around his persona.
I think the war in Iran is about elite loyalty - money and prestige could certainly arise as an opportunity to be taken, but the main reason the US is at war with Iran is to cater to pro-Israel elite in the US.

61949755
if Steiner attacks, everything will be alright.

61950271
>>61942478
>>61942920
Israel wants regime change.
Trump wanted to accomplish that with the same tactics as Venezuela.
Venezuela was a not a regime change per se but was a capitulation that could result in peaceful regime change if the US enforces fair elections.
So they were trying to do something similar, "it's just that easy, kidnap/kill the leader!"
The problem is that the devil is in the details. Maduro was genuinely broadly unpopular, with uprising blocked by Cartels with a profit motive and Cuban Communist ideology. The Cartels fold if they smell profit and the Cuban control was naturally very weak and not sustainable.
Meanwhile the Iranian opposition is not broadly popular, neither is restoring the Shah, and Islamic ideology is not weak.
The protests were not an Arab Spring.
So creating a martyr of the Ayatollah obviously didn't work.
So the leaders are gone, but the regime is still intact, and there is no plan. Just like Iraq, boots on the ground are required for regime change, and Trump would instead prefer to end this quickly and open up the Strait of Hormuz.
Boots on the ground is insanely unpopular, and protracted conflict means oil-induced recession, and Iran would be foolish to agree to any ceasefire.

61950276
Iran will fall and within a year of that, America will fall too. This entire war is engineered to leave Israel as the top dog in the middle east that doesn't have to answer to anyone.

61950277
>>61942181
US govt is nothing more than an international criminal terrorist mafia
And the US military are carrying out acts of terrorism and genocide on behalf of ziocons

61950288
>>61950271
>Regime
Iran has a legitimate government.
The genocidal terrorist states of us and Israel are ruled by criminal mafia 'regimes'

61951750
>>61950277
thanks for the generic retard-commie take, thats a fractional half-truth told to deliberately mislead people. Retard.

>>61950288
religious dictatorships that originally formed from seizing power during a foreign coup, are not legitimate governments. Legitimate governments are ones the majority of the citizens of that country want in power, Iran's leadership are retarded, inbred, genocidal murderers at the best of times. And you're enabling that with your disgusting sycophancy.

There is 1 single positive about Iran, and thats they're not part of the IMF and don't bend the knee to the centraI banking carteI kikes. That's it, the rest is nothing but negatives. They're even more disliked than lsraeI by their neighbours.

61951769
>>61945267
Inverse jim cramer is defunct
Hes right
Seethe more

61951785
>>61942478
>The problems is that the US simply doesn't know what it wants.

The US knows exactly what it wants, to remind people that it holds the biggest stick in the room and to remind them that it controls most of the energy in the world which means it controls most of the world. They want the straight closed, they want countries to suffer, they want hyperinflation so people flock to the dollar and US assets. They want oil prices high enough to make developing venezuela viable. They want to shove a dagger in china's side and show them that they're not ready to invade taiwan yet.

61951788
>>61951785
the 55 IQ tell (and the hint to ignore everything that you dictated to your speak-and-spell software) is your mention of a chinese invasion of taiwan.

61951800
>>61945575
>proffessor chink-chonk ping-pong
Has this monkey been right about anything, even once?

61951807
>>61951788
>China has been salivating over seizing and taking back Taiwan for longer than anyone ITT has been alive
>their media hypes it up as a positive to chinese nationals all the time
>MY countries fake news government propaganda "news" outlets, abide by CCP dictats and refuse to call Taiwan, Taiwan, the Australian's government hack-media call Taiwan Taipei, every single time.
>UN officials wont speak to journalists who ask them about Taiwan at all.

You think China doesn't want to invade Taiwan? are you a chink shilling yourself, or just extremely retarded?

61951811
>>61951788
Whether they actually intend to invade or not doesn't matter, it shows them that the US knows their weakness and will go to any length to exploit it should they attack. The threat of cutting off chips is a serious one and real countries don't just sit back and say 'oh they'll never do it, they would be crazy'. The US just showed you that there are countries that will do crazy things if you get complacent.

61951816
>>61951807
>>61951811
i am replying to individuals who likely have a GED and who earn an $18-24/hour "living" by performing manual labor.

61951851
>>61951816
Engineering degree and $65 an hour at a cushy boomer job with a retirement plan.

61952536
>>61942513
The multimillenarian Sanhedrin is strong in race. Everything is happening as planned. Solomon God King will come, to show the people of the world, what true Strength really is. When the time of the prophecy comes, when all is revealed, the people of the world will rejoice, already knowing who the māšīaḥ is.
Indeed HE who has to come is now HE who has come.
You people rejoice. Death, is not the end.

61952552
How can people even debate this? The US and Israelis are bombing targets with absolute impunity. Iran has no navy, no air defense, no air force... When the targets start drying up, the war is over.

61952615
>>61948937
>Let the anglos leave at Dunkirk, when they were at his mercy.
>Attack Russia, when already fighting against the anglos: Operation Barbarossa. Sure if Napoleon couldn't go further than Moscow, germany, against both the anglos and russia, can do it.
>Two fronts, against two different powerful adversaries, actually the two most powerful: anglos and russia.
>ignore america's production capacity in tanks, planes, trucks boats, because, why not. Which obviously was enter the war for little brother Britain at one point.

61952623
>And that's a nothingburger

61952638
What was the last war the US won anyway? Korea: lost (NK exists and has nukes), Vietnam: lost, Afghanistan: lost, Middle East: speedrunning the loss right now.

61952681
>>61952536
Get ready to get dragged into more sin. Wrong mesiah

item.title: GOD BLESS THIS MAN — 16 replies — 39.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
GOD BLESS THIS MAN

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-08 20:31 UTC

OP

OIL IS SAVED

Early replies (first 3)

61952381
>>61952353
Last chance to make free money by buying oil

61952397
>>61952353
So the strait is going to be closed for years then. Got it.

61952417
>>61952353
What the fuck is political risk insurance?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61952447
>orange retard rugs oil prize
>OIL IS LE SAVED GUYS
ngmi

61952459
>>61952447
the trick is to just say what you think will make you look good. it doesn't matter if it's rue, retards don't fact check

61952462
>>61952417
a bunch of orange nigger nonsense. oil prices are still through the roof.

61952467
>>61952381
You mean by selling oil

61952471
>>61952462
>Political risk insurance is a type of insurance that can be taken out by businesses, of any size, against political risk—the risk that revolution or other political conditions will result in a loss.
How very Jewish.

61952481
>>61952471
again it's just a bunch of orange nigger nonsense. these insurance products might exist but wagies aren't going to risk life and limb on a floating missile/drone target.

61952500
>>61952481
literal third worlders from SEA will risk their lives.

61952509
>>61952353
US govt and the drumpf regime nothing but an international criminal terrorist mafia
Mossad is in control of running the usa

61952600
>>61952353
>>oil has pretty much stabilized
is that stabilisation in the room with us ?

61952614
>>61952471
Uh....so puts on insurance companies then? This sounds just like when banks were forced to give out house loans to niggers and we all know how that went

61952762
>>61952353
Nobody is going to sail a tanker through the Straight of Hormuz on the word of this absolute retard.
He started bombing Iran during peace negotiations. His word isn't worth wiping your arse with.

61952795
>>61952762
He is one of the last honest men left

61952800
>>61952353
Trump should send Barron to help with the Strait clearing operation.

item.title: Is moving away from gold as a store of value inevitable? — 31 replies — 37.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is moving away from gold as a store of value inevitable?

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-08 20:06 UTC

OP

If human technology advances exponentially it wont be long until it is so sophisticated acquiring any type of mineral in large quantities will be trivial. As much as bitcoin gets ragged on with quantum fud atleast there are already contingencies being drawn up to make btc wallets quantum resistant, no such contingences can ever be made for gold or silver. We can already identify asteroids with 10 quintillion dollars worth of gold, what happens when it starts being mined?

PM holders will say the most valuable use case for gold or silver is in the event of total societal collapse, currency debasement and a permanent shut down of the internet. But how much will gold even matter in a world like that? Guns, ammunition and food will ultimately become the most desirable commodities (maybe even just guns and ammo). Your gold will be worthless if you are missing one of the aforementioned three. So until then shouldn't I just stockpile bitcoin, guns, ammo and preserved food?

Early replies (first 3)

61950205
>>61950195
buttcoin is not gonna replace gold, retard

61950211
>>61950195
are you familiar with the idea of post-scarcity?

what's actually going to happen is you're going to exist in a simulation, as part of that simulation. You won't have a body so you won't have any access to physical gold. All the gold in your world will be simulated, and they can simulate infinite gold if they want.

61950544
>>61950205

Could be sooner, could be later, but will, eventually.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950586
>>61950544
>dude, literal nothing is gonna replace a metal created by stars that humans have valued for thousands of years
literally kys. it would do humanity a favor. we can't afford stupidity like this infecting the young generation

61950669
>> muh asteroids.

61950715
>>61950586

If you understood how gold works as money, you'd know that it's true. You don't understand gold.

61952108
>>61950669
>muh fire
>muh agriculture
>muh wheel
>muh bronze tools
>muh printing press
>muh automobile
>muh airplane
>muh internet
>muh artificial intelligence
>muh space mining
>muh quantum computers

61952152
>>61952108
what's the current time estimate until asteroid mining?

61952179
>>61952152
There are concepts of a plan

61952184
>>61950195
Even if there's a massive gold deposit found in some asteroid in space, the cost to mine that asteroid would have to be smaller than the value of the gold mined. Or else no one would mine it.
Think about the costs of the mining equipment, the cost of crews to man them, the cost of the fuel to send a rocket to the meteor, the cost of the ship itself. The man hours on said asteroid. Etc, etc, etc.

It can cost upwards of 100 million dollars to launch a manned rocket into space. Likely even more than that with all the extra stuff you'd have to haul to actually mine the asteroid. Not to mention shipping the gold back and forth is going to cost a metric shit ton. Not to mention gold is extremely dense, which means shipping it is extremely difficult for space travel.
The the current rates of gold and taking into account only the cost of the rocket going and coming, you'd need to bring back 2tons of gold from every trip to break even. That doesn't account for the mining operation cost, the man hour costs, or any other costs. Simply the cost of the rocket.

In short:
You're retarded. The only time such an expedition would be feasible, were if gold costed exponentially more. Which completely defeats the point you're trying to make.

61952199
>>61952152
Current "estimate" is 2040s but who the fuck really knows. We can't know exactly what technological advances will be made and when, all we can really say for sure is productivity will increase. Maybe AI accelerates the efficiency of earth mining, maybe we mine asteroids or maybe we just find some way no-one has even thought of yet.

61952210
>>61952184
>It can cost upwards of 100 million dollars to launch a manned rocket into space

Now, it does. NOW being the most expensive and least efficient it will ever be. In 1980 it cost 10 million dollars to make a "supercomputer" with less computing power than a first gen iphone in 2007. You would do well to understand this.

61952217
>>61950195
Ultimately 99% of all shitcoin "investors" don't see it as an alternate currency free from government control or anything like that. They're just riding the high of wanting to be the next guy to get rich when buttcoin goes to a bajillion dollars, but unfortunately, that ship has sailed. These fake digital coins were invented so Epstein could buy children and then make bank converting it to money when it pumped. Gold and silver are real money

61952227
>>61952184
>tfw you were born in an age without cost effective space travel

61952314
>>61952217

You're an idiot.

Ultimately 99% of stock investors don't see shares as a stake in company or anything like that. They're just riding the high of wanting to be the next guy to get rich when the stock market goes to a bajillion dollars.

> that ship has sailed

You can't predict the market, especially for an asset that you don't even understand.

> fake digital coins

Can you explain this? None of you seem to be able to.

> invented so Epstein could buy children

moron.

61952330
>>61952210
Brother, I was talking about the cost to LAUNCH the rocket. Not manufacture it. The manufacturing cost is around 45m for a SpaceX Falcon 9.
It costs in the ballpark of 100m to LAUNCH the rocket. Then you need to factor in the fact that hauling mining equipment is going to take multiple launches, or significantly larger rockets which will cost more to manufacture and launch.
Then you need to factor in the the cost of the mining equipment, because it won't be efficient to bring it back. Which will be easily over 250m.
So let's just call it 500m, which is 6,250 pounds of gold to break even. But that's vastly underestimating the cost of the rocket and launch, because the rocket alone is probably close to 300m to ship something like that. Then you need a space station, operation costs of the mining station AND the space station. You're looking at 1.5b just to start mining the gold.
Which means you'd need to mine 300,000 OZ of gold at the current value to break even BEFORE YOU EVEN START shipping it back. That also doesn't factor in R&D costs, of a ship and the mining equipment. Nor does it even factor in things like rocket fuel for back and forth, nor does it factor in the electricity to power such a massive mining operation. Nor does it factor in the difficulty of shipping gold in space because of its extreme density. And this all "best case scenario" costs. It could easily cost over a bill for the mining equipment, plus another bill for the rocket plus another 2bill for the station. Plus operating costs and man hours to assemble all that shit.

The only time this would be feasible would be in a world where gold is like 50k/oz. Which again, disproves the claim being made.
>>61952227
We aren't very far from cost effective space travel. The main hinderance is weight. And to ship heavier things you you bigger, heavier rockets. Which makes it exponentially more difficult and costly.

61952339
>>61952184
We already know of one massive gold deposit in this solar system, the asteroid Psyche-16.
But you are correct, it's far too difficult to extract and bring back.

61952347
>>61950195
Land, food, water

61952356
>>61952330
You seem to be entirely misunderstanding the point I was trying to convey to you. All of those things continually become more efficient and cheaper as technology advances. Everything you're saying is under the complete false assumption that efficiency will never increase and operating costs stay the same.

61952387
>>61952314
>Ultimately 99% of stock investors don't see shares as a stake in company or anything like that. They're just riding the high of wanting to be the next guy to get rich when the stock market goes to a bajillion dollars.

What are dividends?

>You can't predict the market, especially for an asset that you don't even understand.

Trust me bro buttcoin is gonna moon to a million just buy more it's what every world govt wants you to do who cares if it loses 50% of its value if a video of trump farting gets posted on xitter

>Can you explain this? None of you seem to be able to.

Can you melt a Bitcoin and turn it into jewelry? Can you melt that jewelry and turn it back into a Bitcoin? Can you dissolve it in acid and hide it on a shelf in a jar in plain sight? Oh it's just ones and zeroes on a screen? I guess its not real and it can just disappear if you don't have access to a computer or the Internet or electricity huh.

> invented so Epstein could buy children

Yeah, Epstein is satoshi and you're probably an Indian who wants old people to buy Bitcoin so you can scam them, kys fagit

61952406
>>61952356
You're missing your own point. It will likely never be economically feasible to mine gold to dump it on the Earth market.
Space rockets will never be mass produced on the same scale as something like computers. And that's what pushes the prices down, mass production.
Having more advanced production is not cheaper, it's more expensive, but it has the benefit of being able to produce larger quantities.
Look at car factories, they're huge and produces hundreds of cars per day. THAT is why cars are cheap.

Rocket factories will never be on that scale, NEVER. Because the average consumer won't be buying a fucking rocket that is capable of sending a large scale multi-billion dollar mining rig to an asteroid to extract one of the most difficult ores to ship due to its density. Use your fucking brain man. For 0.1 seconds.
The cost of everything trends upwards. Operation costs trend upward, energy costs trend upward, employment cost trends upward. Cost of raw materials trend upward.
And even if there came a day where we started mining gold in space, it wouldn't be to transport it back to earth. It would be for use in space to avoid the cost associated with shipping it from Earth.

You sound like a kid that played no man's sky once and thought "WOW WE COULD TOTALLY DO THIS IRL FOR REAL AND MAKE BIG DOLLAR MONEY MONEY SIGNS"

61952414
>>61952387

>What are dividends?
Dividends are a fucking meme, they are useful only when you already have a bajillion dollars. No working person is DCA'ing their retirement into high dividend etfs, its always high growth broad market equities that don't pay shit in terms of yield.

>Can you melt a Bitcoin and turn it into jewelry?
KEK, this is what shiny rock holders always default to. Can a nigger wear it on his teeth? No? guess it has no value then, my semitic jewler told me so.

>I guess its not real and it can just disappear if you don't have access to a computer or the Internet or electricity huh.
If you don't have access to the internet or electricity then its the apocalypse you dumb nigger. No one will care how many shiny rocks you have when they are going to be shooting you in the head and taking your food.

>you're probably an Indian
Projection.

61952430
>>61952414
You are right. They are literal manchilds dreaming of the next pipe dream that's just two weeks away.

If it was feasible to mine raw materials in space, it was also feasible to mine them in the oceans. Guess why it isn't? It's not profitable to do so, that's why.

61952435
>>61952387

> What are dividends?

What is staking?

> Can you melt a Bitcoin and turn it into jewelry?

Do you think this is why we've used gold as money?

> Can you dissolve it in acid and hide it on a shelf in a jar in plain sight?

Not necessary. It's invisible and intrinsically secure.

> I guess its not real
> the internet and software aren't real, stay away from investing in Google, Microsoft, Apple, and Faceebook

> it can just disappear if you don't have access to a computer access to a computer or the Internet or electricity huh

Do think that you're going to be able to vastly overpay for things and draw attention to your resources at the gas station, when the electricity and internet go out?

> Yeah, Epstein is satoshi

You're a moron.

61952443
>>61952430
Meant for >>61952406 of course.

61952477
>>61952406

>Rocket factories will never be on that scale, NEVER. Because the average consumer won't be buying a fucking rocket
The average consumer is not buying heavy construction machinery or airplanes that doesn't mean they aren't manufactured at scale by assembly lines. What the fuck are you even talking about man? China is already starting to mass produce reuseable space rockets. This point alone just demonstrates to me you have very little understanding on this topic yet speak authoritatively on it.

61952541
>>61952414
Making over $40 in dividends in 5 years on a small retail account is not a nothing burger. Especially when the first 2 years I was only getting pennies. I would be a little in red for total profits if it were not for dividends. That is how you make money off of stocks that crab most of the time. Some of them if you just hold for 15 or 20 years you get all your money back in dividends. I did the math on the highest paying ones. These are not ETFs. I own 5 stocks that pay 2% or more.

61952560
>>61950195
Ok lets estimate the odds here

>Asteroid mining
0.01% chance that this happens

>Gold still being used
99.9% chance that this happens

61952582
>>61952477
You don't have a single idea what you're talking about, do you?
China produces 25 reusable rockets a year, that's 2 a month. Does that sound like mass production to you? Rockets that cannot even break even on transporting the ores. They cannot even land on an asteroid. So you'd need a super specific space station for it to dock on.

Gold will probably never be profitable to mine in space with the intent of delivering it back to earth. If we do mine gold in space, it will be for space use to avoid the extreme shipping costs of shipping something so dense.
And all the nearby asteroids which we could mine aren't gold.
>>61952430
I have no idea why this guy is so attached to the idea of some giga-billionaire burning billions and billions of dollars just so he can dump space-mined gold onto the earth market at a massive loss to crash the price of gold.
Makes about zero sense.

I mean, maybe it makes sense in 2000 years when some planet tycoon ultra bazillionaire sees that earths gold price is significantly higher and he can profit off that, but even then...

61952616
>>61952414
>>61952435

61952741
>>61950195
>Your gold will be worthless
something that never happend in human history will happen in two more weeks

Half the people I know don’t even understand the scope of the war we just started. To them, it’s a couple stories they swipe past on their social media feeds to watch midget fights, they’re literal mindless cattle and their taxes allow these wars.

OP

61950228

61950235

61950239

>I totally vooooted for Trump first term but now I am a pissed off former MAGA

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61951201
>>61950850
Or maybe he grew up and realized that you can't be an unhinged, unprofessional, tantrum-throwing kike lover and be the president of the most powerful country on the planet. Grow up, dude. The innocence of 2016 is never coming back. We fucked up big time, and the people that will be affected the most by this will be our children.

61951204
>>61950940
Dodging the draft is one of the very few based things that he did, you bootlicking faggot lol

61951313
>>61950220
European "leaders" of the nowadays are completely indefensible.
I say that as a European, that unfortunately lives in a EU nation.
Starmer, Sanchez, Macron, Merz...it's all the same bunch, that constantly makes decisions against the best interests of their people.
...and then they act surprised, that the oppositional parties are rising, which is ADANGERTOOURDEMOCRACY. No, it's simply FAFO and you will FO, whenever the voters get to make a choice.

61951335
>>61950947
This. And Trump has made that obvious to everyone with more than a few functioning brain cells.

61951336
>>61950228
He's rambling like a madman, they need to get him off the internet, its retarded that POTUS is talking shit on other countries 24/7.

61951363
>>61951313
The opposition parties are secretely the same bunch of assholes. They don't care about the people or interests of the nation, they just want to be the ones in charge who get all the sweet bribes just like the current leader. It's two sides of the same coin situation and any politician with the will to actually go against the system is immediately called a russian agent and ostracized from politics.

61951396
>>61951363
Farage, LePen and Weidel would be a much-needed upgrade for Europe. (No idea who that would be in Spain, but I didn't know Sanchez either until he declared to give hundreds-of-thousands of refugees the Spanish citizenship, so they can freely relocate within the EU.)
And as long as the establishment doesn't get them banned for wrongthink, they will probably rule in due time.
Unlike the usual Socialist/"""Conservative""" revolving door-style change-of-government, they are distinctly different, which is why they attract so much use of governmental power against them. The parties-of-old genuinely fear them.
Obviously can't vouch 100% for them, but they look like the only way out of this mess for Europe.

61951439
Americans are so fucking stupid. They deserve everything coming to them

61951451
>>61951091
Lmao obsessed mutt.

61951456
>>61950220
it's funny because mutts mocking europoors for being slave imigrants cucksuckers, yet they have pedo president who starts blasting wars left and right, just to cover up his "love" to children. Amerimutts, say hi to your pedo jewish overlords.

61951458
>>61951396
Kek what mess? The mess of Russia not being able to just invade countries it desires, and the mess of USA not being able to just dictate what europe does? It’s funny how you chuds are simply too stupid to see your own benefit, instantly falling to foreign propaganda. Both Russia and USA want to see the EU gone, but this doesn’t raise any questions in your retarded brain?

61951481
>>61951456
>assertions without proof
Nice fanfiction. Meanwhile there's literal videos of Biden molesting children on camera and you shills say nothing. Hypocrites.

61951489
>>61951481
The Dow is at 50k and trump wants to bang is own daughter and definitely diddled kids on Epsteins island .

61951493
>>61951458
(Among other things) the mess of uncontrolled mass migration. The people of the European nations are decisively against it.
Their only option on the ballot to stop that are the aforementioned oppositional powers, because the parties-of-old stand united against their people.
Now, make no mistake, I live in a town that has been having a large number of non-European foreigners since the late 60s/early 70s.
Even the migrants from back then have had it with the newer migrants (especially since 2015, but also since Bulgaria and Romania became EU nations).
They do not learn the local language, they can not provide for themselves, they are disproportionally criminal and some of them even hate the local people.
The parties-of-old (and the EU under their unchecked control) will fight for these guys to the blood. What the flying fuck do they expect the voters to do in light of that? The parties-of-old are the extremist forces, not the ones trying to represent the will of the people.

61951509
>>61951493
I agree that migration needs to be regulated but it’s no excuse to fucking dismantle all of europe and leave it naked for other great powers.

61951513
>>61951509
It's not about "dismantling Europe", though.
It's about turning the EU into a Europe of sovereign nations, so that the people are back in control instead of a completely detached "elite" in Brussels, that is effectively entirely unaccountable.

61951518
>>61950239
bad case of TDS (trump dicksucking syndrome)

61951603
>>61950947
That is correct. But it also has one master.

61951606
>>61951603
The master is just the biggest parasite.

61951633
How is this not bullish?

61951910
>>61950947
strange thing to call your jewish overlords parasites. Kek

61951932
>>61950850
do you also laugh at the absurdity of another man's dick entering your ass in an ironic and non-gay manner?

61951967
Taco incoming

61952008
>>61951091
>US should be carpet bombing european and EU govts and Israle, not iran
*Ftfy

61952070
>>61951458
The fact that you're posting about EU rather than your own actual country is already proof that your country is such a mess you don't even want to be associated with it. You have to group dozens of failed countries together to make something resembling a powerful entity and you think nobody's noticing what you're doing. You're the only one not noticing.
A decade ago "EU nationalists" like you didn't exist, now they're in every thread. That's how bad things have gotten on the level of individual EU countries

61952104
>>61951967
I can definitely sense hes going to cuck out way before the given timeline and randomly declare victory after oil reaches a certain price, maybe $120. That jew-owned cumslut always does this

61952110
>>61951633

61952147
>>61951458
These politicians had over 10 years to realize the US and Russia aren't friends and do something about it and they did jack shit. The current mainstream parties aren't worth it, they are useless.

61952325
>>61950220

61952521
I guess it is just dipping before the post-war momster pump.

item.title: The US has 10 days — 108 replies — 18.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 108 • Updated: 2026-03-08 15:12 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61942707
I don't mind if Israel and the US want to be retarded in their own little sandboxes, but why do they have to drag the rest of the world into it

61942764
>>61938506
The only thing that matters is that the US is the first to achieve AGI. >>61938487
Is on to something here. This move, which seems to be such a blunder, is the only thing that can set China and the EU back enough for the US to win the AI race.

61942831
>>61938341
>The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz
No.
>or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)
Nobody cares.
> on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day.
Ok, sot there's 1/4 of all oil that isn't getting to EU and UK.
So, oil price will go up.
>Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW
It'll be $100 - 150 .
I guess EU and UK should have not sanctioned Russia, tough luck now.

61942837
>>61938345
Wholly retard.
Trump made US oil independent during first term, and US is top 3 oil producing country now.
Oil price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget.

61942844
>>61942831

BOND YIELDS will crash the economy, not oil itself. With a structural 15m daily barrel deficit oil will go up every week. The only way to destroy demand is recession, oil will go up until it reaches the breaking point for global growth and blows everything up.

There is no feelings or opinions to this. Shutting the strait is a 15m barrel deficit. Ask any energy expert that that does to prices. It’s 150-200

61942850
>>61942837

Has nothing to do with US energy production because crude is a global market. Just ask yourself what would happen if the 30 year yield went to 7%. That’s a 30-45% dump in equities

61942851
>>61942837
I'm sure Joe Shcmo on the street is going to be happy paying 2 dollars a litre to fund Israel. Do you NEET retards really live in such a vacuum?

61942854
Crypto will pump. The thesis for crypto has always been based on the collapse of the system

61942897
>>61942854
Crypto is fiat with extra steps
No fiat, no crypto

61944577
One week in so far. WTI: 65-90
If this continue another week we will see 125 WTI and markets down 5%

61944640
>>61938341
>What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.
So the XRP domino theory is playing out?
Bullish for XRP?

61945025
>>61938402
>Anything bigger than that is already dead
It was literally yesterday that Iran launched a Khoramshahr-4, the biggest missile in their arsenal, at Tel Aviv

61945048
>>61942837
The US oil sector is small compared to the rest of the US economy. It is privately owned, so the bulk of profits go to private shareholders, not the government.

61945281
>>61938425
If you are good at reading body language you can see when they are together Bibi has nothing but contempt for Trump, it is because bibi has seen all the VHS tapes, he knows he owns his ass and treats him like a dog to do his bidding

61945399
>>61939987
More prideful of their shah maybe, only retards give a shit about the Ayatollah.

61945481
This Is the end.
Bye bye USA.
Bye bye israHell

61945896
>>61938341
Yep it's exactly what I voted for.

61948489
>>61945481
trust the plan, patriots are in control

61950994
>>61938341
bump, keeping this thread alive till day 10

61951019
How do I become a hormuzi oil runner with a crew of desperadoes slipping the hot zone to deliver uninsured cargo at great profit? I have no maritime experience, vessel, capital, or connections btw.

61951156
>>61938341
You are max doom posting. It's almost like you believe there is no solution to whatever problem you come up with.

80% of the oil that passes through the strait is destined for Asia/China, and they let some of it pass so it's not even as significant as you make it out to be.
Oil prices won't affect rate decision in May and beyond because the next FED chair promised cuts to Trump and that's all that matters to the markets.
Bad job numbers, 2.5 CPI and unemployment rate already justify cuts. FOMC members understand that the inflation caused by oil prices is transitory and they are willing to ignore it to help the economy. Federal spending will grow just like it did at the time of the Iraq war.

It's likely that the markets continue to fall in the coming weeks but as the war drags on, the negative effects wear off as the reality of the situation becomes priced in and Iran's firepower gradually diminishes.

t. longed USOIL and shorted SPX several weeks ago already

61951171
>>61951156
>they let some of it pass
Not by choice. Not that a few tankers can make up for a huge chunk of the global supply being cut off for weeks if not months. Nobody cares about what it will be at a year from now. If my options go up I’ll multiply my portfolio a few times in a week or two like I did with silver and oil tankers already this year and cash out most of my profits.

61951173
>>61938341
>The only way is to put troops on the ground.
Iran literally said they'll stop shooting if US coalition stops shooting. So maybe try doing that

61951183
>>61951156
>Iran's firepower gradually diminishes
That's the expectation but nobody takes into account that China can just supply weapons to Iran infinitely. Just like nobody took into account that China/Russia could supply intel to Iran. The precedent for both of those is already established in Ukraine, China wouldn't be crossing any red lines NATO didn't already cross

61951189
>>61951156
>Oil prices won't affect rate decision in May and beyond because the next FED chair promised cuts to Trump and that's all that matters to the markets.
There's 12 voting members of the FOMC you dumb nigger, the Fed Chair is just a spokesman he has no more power than any of the other voting members of the FOMC

61951218
>>61951189
Already know that, retard, and it changes nothing.

The FED Chair has significant influence and can convince other governors and regional presidents to align with his vote. Opposition is uncommon.
There are already a few members that are aligned with Trump's preferences.

Trump also replaced the BLS commissioner.

61951230
>>61951218
The Fed Chair only has the influence he can create, there will be no rate cuts

61951231
>>61938502
> advantage of the already strapped consumer yet again
I am a fan of the growing wealth gap

61951744
>>61938502
It does though. Many American allies, especially in Asia, depend on ME oil and are not likely to be happy about America slamming their economies into recession. And there are American allies in the ME that are dependent on their oil exports and they are already getting fucked. Do you think Arab countries that hate Israel to begin with are going to stand still for having America fuck them over for the benefit of Israel? Countries like the UAE are in dire straits already. They have 100 billion dollars in US treasuries. Think they won't dump them on the market?

61951852
>>61951156
waiting on the Fed, job numbers, regular babble etc

"one of them faggots"
stick your job numbers in your apple pie

item.title: How high will OIL go? — 31 replies — 17.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How high will OIL go?

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-08 19:06 UTC

OP

150?
200?

Early replies (first 3)

61950050
>>61949961
The Trump admin probably starts trying to implement price controls above $150.

61950059
>>61949961
10,000 Gold.

61950077
How will crypto perform without rate cuts in the next 3 years?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950083
>>61950059
Eoy yes

61950184
>>61949961
That's Tehran right? So all those oil tanks were holding the oil that Iran was using to pay for those non-working radars and anti-ship missiles. Winnie the Pooh won't be happy about that.

61950223
I knew I should have bought an oil container when it was like 2.50 a gallon a little while ago but I had no idea how that would even work

61950468
>>61949961
The only thing keeping oil high is insurance companies. When they start insuring again oil will be flowing through the Strait again and oil will hit its top next week.

61950713
>>61949961
Alright, let's look at where this might go...

1973 Oil Crisis:

In March 1974, OAPEC lifted the embargo, but the price of oil had risen by nearly 300%: from US$3 per barrel to nearly US$12 per barrel globally. Prices in the United States were significantly higher than the global average.

- $3 -> $12 = 400% increase

- In today's dollars: $22.91 -> $83.76, 365.6% increase (which coincidentally also shows how retarded the way the gov calculates inflation is)

1979 Oil Crisis:

In January 1979, the U.S. crude oil first purchase price was approximately $9.46 per barrel. "Starting with the Iranian revolution, the price of crude oil rose to $39.50 per barrel over the next 12 months (its all-time highest real price until March 3, 2008)."

- $9.46 -> $39.50 = 417.5% increase

- In today's dollars: $45.05 -> $157.06, 348.6% increase

Oil's lowest price per barrel over the past year was $56.52, occurring on Dec 19th, 2025. If we multiply that by 400% we get a potential $226.08/barrel.

The price per barrel three weeks ago before it was completely obvious that the US was committed to attacking Iran was $60.71/barrel. 400% × $60.71 = $242.84/barrel.

61950718
>>61950184
The chinese tech from 2004? Imagine using that
>>61949961
They sell like 85% of that oil to middlemen who then sell the oil to China so not a big deal

61950728
>>61950713
(cont.)
A study recently done by Kansas State University found:

"Longer term, the price increase could be 4.2 cents per gallon for every dollar per barrel"

$226 × $0.042 = $9.49/gallon
$243 × $0.042 = $10.20/gallon

If we take the lowest percentage (bent by the gov's inflation calculator) of 348.6% and multiply that by the lowest price per barrel in the past year of $56.52, we get $197.03/barrel translating to $8.28/gallon.

Problems with stocking up on gas right now:

The gasoline supply in the US will remain the winter formulation until the beginning of May. Winter gas has a higher Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP), meaning it evaporates more easily than summer gas. This is less conducive to long-term storage. While people often claim that the RVP is so the gas ignites easier in winter, in reality the different formulation is mostly due to cost and air pollution concerns - in the winter, you can get away with cheaper formulations because the cold temps lead to less air pollution. Being more stabile, summer gas is better for storage any time of the year. Fuel stabilizers don't really improve this particular aspect of winter gas.

Various taxes, regulatory measures and subsidized incentives may also be implemented. Towards the end of the Carter administration, domestic oil was reduced to about 1/5th the global price per barrel through various measures including a windfall profit tax on oil companies which basically erased their profit margins from the high global prices keeping oil prices in the US for US use lower than elsewhere in the world. In 1980 the US imported about 40% of the oil it used so this didn't lower the price of gas by 4/5ths, but it did blunt the price felt by consumers. These efforts resulted in the price of gas only doubling instead of quadrupling. If gas prices only doubled from their pre-war average of ~$2.90/gal we'd see ~$5.80/gal.

61950790
>>61950728
>If gas prices only doubled from their pre-war average of ~$2.90/gal we'd see ~$5.80/gal.
a literal nothingburger.

BUYING GAS IS THE CHEAPEST PART OF OWNING A CAR!

61950801
>>61950728
>$8.28/gallon.
I checked and its higher then that in Germany.

61950847
>>61950801
My math was for the US only. As the world's largest producer of oil and now an oil exporting country, we have it better than most other countries.

61950857
>>61949961
No one cares about oil prices anymore, chud.

61950868
>>61950790
Driving a little gasoline Toyota Corolla 100,000 miles @ 40mpg and $5.80/gal: $14,500

Driving a larger electric Toyota bZ4X 100,000 miles at 252 miles per charge and $15/charge: $5,952

Look at what happened to the motor vehicle market after the 1973 and 1979 oil crises. Definitely not a nothingburger for investors.

61950872
>>61950857
Didn't Tesla stop making cars?

61950875
>>61950872
>tesla is the only ev maker
>>61950868
>still not owning an ev

61951080
>>61950184
>non-working radars
Funny that you say that

61951096
>>61949961
What oil stock do I buy bros????

61951236
>>61951080
Here is a real image, unlike your lazy well poisoning attempt
https://edition.cnn.com/2026/03/05/middleeast/radar-bases-us-missile-defense-iran-war-intl-invs

61951283
>>61949961
What will happen on Monday bros..?

61951306
>>61951283
Oil go up

61951325
>>61949961
At this point, over 300. Short term I'm guessing around 150.

61951346
>>61949961
I still don't understand why Americans give a blood sacrifice to Israel every 10 years. Is it some sexual thing?

61951350
>>61949961

This is the biggest oil production cut in the history of mankind. I don’t think an initial spike to $300 before face melting deflation kicks in is out of the question

61951441
>>61951346
It is a sexual thing and it's on tape.

61952245
>>61950875
>still not owning an ev

In the 90s and 2000s my dad was 100% against cell phones, despite cell phones having been around in some form since the 70s. He grudgingly finally got one for business around 2010. Now he doesn't have a home landline. You'll huff and puff for another decade while the world moves on from internal combustion vehicles, but you'll eventually own one like everyone else. Every oil crisis has accelerated the replacement of less efficient vehicles with more efficient vehicles, and we've maxxed out the efficiency of gasoline engines. New battery technology is already doubling range and longevity while halving charge time over what was possible just a couple of years ago. People are running out of excuses to avoid EVs and reasons to keep ICEs. So, you can be the modern equivalent of a guy in the 1930s still riding a horse into town and rejecting those new fangled automobiles, but from an investor standpoint EVs and the technology surrounding them are where the growth is and will continue to be. Personally, I look forward to a world without jeet-owned gas stations and white trash greasemonkey youtube slop.

61952254
>>61951441

oh vey

61952315
>>61952245
Yeah you want overpriced golf carts with planned obsolescence instead. Such an improvement!

61952559
>>61950713
>>61950728
Based and rare high analysis anon, Im doing an investigation currently myself but it’s difficult without drawing from past source’s because the current MSM story is far removed from reality even from a cursory glance. Need to keep eyes on the situation as it unravels, but it seems you’re indicating that this is more than a short spike and could be a year long event?

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 23 replies — 16.4/hr

Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-03-08 19:31 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

item.title: We were promised total collapse — 8 replies — 9.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
We were promised total collapse

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-08 20:05 UTC

OP

When will the American economy completely collapse? It's a FIAT economy run with fractional reserve banking

Early replies (first 3)

61950867
>>61950814
That lil' faggot was a hero.
Imagine what could be achieved, if we weren't a bunch of cattle pussy:
Total glownigger annihilation.

61950955
>>61950814
>a FIAT economy run with fractional reserve banking
You don't get it. Murricans work more and harder than slaves. They eat the slop. The are all in debt paying interest, both via the state and privately. They are selling what once was their land. They are willing to destroy and pollute the land just like the Chinese. On top of all this they serve zog funding wars (with interest paying debt ofc) and sacrificing their sons to serve in them. The US economy is not collapsing, it is feasting on the sweat, toil, blood and tears of its servants.

61950986
Two more weeks, trust the plan

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950992
>>61950955
